findings.issue: Flag RE-ARM trigger is unspecified, which can reintroduce the very bug the fix targets. The fix correctly replaces the nil-slice first-tick signal with an explicit verboseFirstTick flag ('set on first verbose collect-all-three, cleared after the first tick'). But it never pins down HOW the flag re-arms for a SECOND verbose session. Verified seam: the verbose-toggle viewCh push must NOT call c.Reset() (Decision 2 / tech-spec risk row), while the screen-switch viewCh path calls c.Reset() unconditionally on every push (top/stat.go:108). If verboseFirstTick re-arms only via Reset(), then toggling verbose OFF then ON (no Reset) leaves it false -> the second verbose session's first frame shows a deceptive 0 instead of n/a -> exactly the regression the fix exists to prevent. Task 07 defers Reset() coordination to Task 09, but Task 09 only addresses 'view-switch / Reset() must not leave a stuck throttle-state', not the verbose-OFF->ON re-arm. Since the set/clear logic lives in Task 07, the re-arm trigger must be pinned there.
findings.fix: In Task 07, make re-arm explicit and independent of Reset(): keep a 'was the verbose branch active last tick' bit and set verboseFirstTick whenever the branch is entered after an inactive tick (covers initial enable AND OFF->ON re-enable), clearing it after that single tick. State this in What-to-do / Edge-cases so Task 09's Reset() handling is not the sole re-arm path.

findings.issue: Task 07 TDD anchor and tech-spec testing strategy claim the new live-PG test uses 'the t.Skipf guard pattern, as in existing stat-tests'. Verified: the existing stat tests (TestCollector_Update at internal/stat/stat_test.go:27, TestCollector_collectDiskstats at :71) do NOT skip without a cluster - they assert.NoError on postgres.NewTestConnect(), so they FAIL rather than skip. No t.Skip exists anywhere under internal/stat/*_test.go. The 'as in existing stat-tests' reference is inaccurate; the real convention is connect-or-fail. (Carried over from the prior batch scope; orthogonal to the fix.)
findings.fix: Drop the 't.Skipf as in existing stat-tests' phrasing, or have TestCollector_Update_Verbose explicitly t.Skip when NewTestConnect errors and note it diverges from the existing connect-or-fail tests.

findings.issue: Task 07 cites collectDiskstats/collectNetdevs prev=curr-before-count*Usage at '~303-331'. Exact anchors: prev=curr swap is at internal/stat/stat.go:307-309 (disk) and :327-329 (net); the switch is at 156-175 and Collector snapshot fields at 57-64 (both cited correctly). The mechanism described is accurate; only the disk/net swap line range is slightly loose.
findings.fix: Optional: tighten the citation to 307-309 / 327-329. Non-blocking.
